Now I have comprehensive information from the official documentation. Let me compile a concise comparison report.\n\n---\n\n## Claude Code Configuration Best Practices Report\n\n### 1. CLAUDE.md Files\n\n**Official Best Practice:**\n- **Global scope** (`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`): Personal preferences, coding style, personal tools\n- **Project scope** (`.claude/CLAUDE.md`): Team-shared guidelines, architecture, build commands, coding standards\n- **Local scope** (`.claude.local.md`): Sandbox URLs, personal test data; gitignored\n- **Managed** (admin-deployed): Organization policies, compliance, security\n\n**Size & Structure:**\n- Target under 200 lines per file — longer files consume context and reduce adherence\n- Use markdown headers and path-scoped `.claude/rules/` files for large projects\n- Specific, concrete instructions work best (\"use 2-space indentation\" vs \"format properly\")\n\n**Common Mistakes:**\n- Mixing personal preferences into project CLAUDE.md (pollutes shared context)\n- Storing large reference material inline instead of using `@path` imports or skills\n- Conflicting instructions across multiple CLAUDE.md files\n- Vague rules that are hard to verify\n\n---\n\n### 2. Hooks\n\n**Recommended Hook Patterns:**\n- **SessionStart**: Load development context (branch, uncommitted changes, open issues) — keep fast\n- **PreToolUse**: Block dangerous operations (rm -rf, destructive commands) by inspecting input before execution\n- **PostToolUse**: Run validation after file writes (linting, type-checking)\n- **Stop**: Provide feedback or continue conversation (can block to prevent stopping)\n\n**Hook Configuration Hierarchy** (precedence order):\n1. Managed policy settings (admin-controlled)\n2. `.claude/settings.local.json` (project-specific, gitignored)\n3. `.claude/settings.json` (project-shared)\n4. `~/.claude/settings.json` (all projects)\n\n**Pitfalls:**\n- Long-running hooks slow down every session start\n- Using hooks for guidance instead of enforcement (CLAUDE.md is better for behavioral rules)\n- Conflicting matchers across hook files\n- Exit code confusion: exit 0 = no decision; exit 2 = blocking error\n\n---\n\n### 3. Memory System\n\n**Architecture:**\n- **CLAUDE.md**: Instructions you write, loaded in full at session start, survives compaction\n- **Auto memory**: Claude-written notes saved to `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md`\n- **Auto memory loading**: First 200 lines or 25KB of `MEMORY.md` loaded per session; topic files loaded on-demand\n- **Scope**: Per-project (all worktrees + subdirectories of same repo share one memory directory)\n\n**Best Practices:**\n- Use CLAUDE.md for facts, standards, architecture that don't change\n- Let auto memory accumulate learnings (build commands, debugging patterns, preferences)\n- Keep CLAUDE.md concise — auto memory handles growing context naturally\n- Use `/memory` command to audit what's stored\n\n**Common Mistakes:**\n- Putting procedural instructions in CLAUDE.md instead of skills (skills load on-demand)\n- Assuming auto memory syncs across machines (it doesn't — local only)\n- Treating CLAUDE.md as \"enforced\" (it's context, not enforcement — use hooks for blocking)\n\n---\n\n### 4. MCP Configuration\n\n**Scope:**\n- **User scope** (`~/.claude.json`): Personal integrations, tools you use across projects\n- **Project scope** (`.mcp.json`): Team-required servers (GitHub, memory, monitoring)\n- **Managed settings**: Organization allowlists/denylists\n\n**Best Practices:**\n- Keep `.mcp.json` in version control for team consistency\n- Use allowlists (`allowedMcpServers`) to restrict which servers developers can add\n- Use denylists (`deniedMcpServers`) to block specific servers (takes precedence)\n- Enable approval for project MCP servers: `enableAllProjectMcpServers: true`\n\n**Common Mistakes:**\n- Committing sensitive API keys in `.mcp.json` (use env vars and secrets management)\n- Mixing personal MCPs into project `.mcp.json` (pollutes team setup)\n- Not reviewing MCP server permissions before adding\n\n---\n\n### 5. Skills\n\n**How Skills Load:**\n- Skills activate automatically when Claude detects relevance to the task\n- Triggered explicitly with `/skill-name`\n- Skills only load content when invoked, unlike CLAUDE.md (on-demand)\n- Support YAML frontmatter for invocation control (user-only, auto, never)\n\n**Best Practices:**\n- Use skills for multi-step procedures, checklists, reference material\n- Use CLAUDE.md for facts and rules that apply to every session\n- Create skill when CLAUDE.md entry becomes a procedure (not a fact)\n- Organize with `.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` + supporting files\n\n**Common Mistakes:**\n- Moving large reference material to CLAUDE.md instead of skills (wastes context)\n- Ambiguous activation triggers (specify when/why Claude should invoke)\n- Skills that don't include clear example usage or expected output format\n\n---\n\n### Summary: When to Use Each\n\n| Use Case | Mechanism |\n|----------|-----------|\n| Build commands, must-know facts | CLAUDE.md |\n| Growing learnings over time | Auto memory |\n| Block dangerous commands | PreToolUse hook |\n| Multi-step procedures, checklists | Skills |\n| Team integrations (GitHub, DB) | `.mcp.json` |\n| Personal preferences, tools | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json` |\n\n**Key Rule:** Smaller, more specific CLAUDE.md → better adherence. Move bulk content to skills, rules, or auto memory."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a5be09489d07c676c (use SendMessage with to: 'a5be09489d07c676c' to continue this agent)\n<usage>subagent_tokens: 97708\ntool_uses: 6\nduration_ms: 56044</usage>"}]}]},"uuid":"1e50dff7-da98-45e0-bcf4-ece378be89c6","timestamp":"2026-06-08T05:05:20.277Z","toolUseResult":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Research the current best practices for Claude Code configuration. I need a concise comparison report (under 400 words) covering:\n\n1. CLAUDE.md best practices — what should be in global vs project-level CLAUDE.md, recommended structure, what to avoid\n2. Hooks — recommended hook patterns (SessionStart, Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact), any pitfalls\n3. Memory system — how it works, best practices for memory files\n4. MCP configuration — global vs project-level MCPs, recommended setup\n5. Skills — how they load, any best practices\n\nFocus on: what the official docs or community recommends vs common mistakes.
